understanding.goal_understanding: The objective was to solve each level within a limited step budget by moving the selectable piece(s) on the 3px-per-cell grid to achieve the level’s win condition, typically involving getting a selectable onto a goal tile and/or arranging blocks appropriately.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Positions were given as pixel coordinates [x,y] where each move shifted the currently selected piece exactly one cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction. If a pushable block was immediately adjacent in the movement direction and space allowed, the block would be pushed along one cell with the mover; otherwise movement would fail if blocked by an immovable object such as a wall. Walls acted as barriers that could prevent movement/pushing depending on their placement/behavior. Multiple selectables could exist, and control could be switched at any time using CLICK with the exact pixel-coordinate position of another selectable; CLICK instantly changed which piece subsequent MOVE actions applied to.
